slides.title: PhD CET Mock Exam 6 - Computer Science
slides.subtitle: 50 Practice MCQs for Doctoral Entrance Examination • Topic 1-5

slides.question: Q1: If a research scenario or project requires: **compound proposition builders**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of compound proposition builders, you should select **Propositional Logic Connectives**.

slides.question: Q2: If a research scenario or project requires: **predicate variable scoping symbols**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of predicate variable scoping symbols, you should select **Predicate Logic Quantifiers**.

slides.question: Q3: If a research scenario or project requires: **set manipulation mathematical operations**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of set manipulation mathematical operations, you should select **Set Theory Operations**.

slides.question: Q4: If a research scenario or project requires: **partitioning reflexive-symmetric-transitive relation**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of partitioning reflexive-symmetric-transitive relation, you should select **Equivalence Relation**.

slides.question: Q5: If a research scenario or project requires: **reflexive-antisymmetric-transitive ordering framework**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of reflexive-antisymmetric-transitive ordering framework, you should select **Partially Ordered Set (Poset)**.

slides.question: Q6: If a research scenario or project requires: **vertex incident edge count**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of vertex incident edge count, you should select **Vertex Degree**.

slides.question: Q7: If a research scenario or project requires: **single-visit edge traversal trail**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of single-visit edge traversal trail, you should select **Euler Path**.

slides.question: Q8: If a research scenario or project requires: **single-visit vertex traversal loop**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of single-visit vertex traversal loop, you should select **Hamiltonian Cycle**.

slides.question: Q9: If a research scenario or project requires: **closure-associativity-identity-inverse algebraic structure**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of closure-associativity-identity-inverse algebraic structure, you should select **Group Theory Group**.

slides.question: Q10: If a research scenario or project requires: **order-sensitive selection arrangement**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of order-sensitive selection arrangement, you should select **Permutation**.

slides.question: Q11: If a research scenario or project requires: **process lifecycle stage indicators**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of process lifecycle stage indicators, you should select **Process States**.

slides.question: Q12: If a research scenario or project requires: **ready queue CPU allocation**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of ready queue CPU allocation, you should select **CPU Scheduling**.

slides.question: Q13: If a research scenario or project requires: **prevention of race conditions**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of prevention of race conditions, you should select **Process Synchronization**.

slides.question: Q14: If a research scenario or project requires: **coffman condition elimination strategy**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of coffman condition elimination strategy, you should select **Deadlock Prevention**.

slides.question: Q15: If a research scenario or project requires: **safe-state resource allocation simulator**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of safe-state resource allocation simulator, you should select **Banker's Algorithm**.

slides.question: Q16: If a research scenario or project requires: **elimination of external fragmentation**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of elimination of external fragmentation, you should select **Paging**.

slides.question: Q17: If a research scenario or project requires: **logical memory expansion**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of logical memory expansion, you should select **Virtual Memory**.

slides.question: Q18: If a research scenario or project requires: **fast page table lookup cache**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of fast page table lookup cache, you should select **Translation Lookaside Buffer (TLB)**.

slides.question: Q19: If a research scenario or project requires: **physical memory page eviction policy**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of physical memory page eviction policy, you should select **Page Replacement Algorithm**.

slides.question: Q20: If a research scenario or project requires: **excessive paging performance collapse**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of excessive paging performance collapse, you should select **Thrashing**.

slides.question: Q21: If a research scenario or project requires: **O(log n) average search time**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of O(log n) average search time, you should select **Binary Search Tree (BST)**.

slides.question: Q22: If a research scenario or project requires: **single-source shortest path optimization**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of single-source shortest path optimization, you should select **Dijkstra's Algorithm**.

slides.question: Q23: If a research scenario or project requires: **memoization and tabulation paradigm**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of memoization and tabulation paradigm, you should select **Dynamic Programming**.

slides.question: Q24: If a research scenario or project requires: **O(1) average lookup complexity**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of O(1) average lookup complexity, you should select **Hashing**.

slides.question: Q25: If a research scenario or project requires: **efficient priority queue implementation**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of efficient priority queue implementation, you should select **Heap Data Structure**.

slides.question: Q26: If a research scenario or project requires: **strictly balanced binary search tree**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of strictly balanced binary search tree, you should select **AVL Tree**.

slides.question: Q27: If a research scenario or project requires: **in-place sorting with O(n log n) average time**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of in-place sorting with O(n log n) average time, you should select **Quick Sort**.

slides.question: Q28: If a research scenario or project requires: **space-efficient sparse graph representation**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of space-efficient sparse graph representation, you should select **Adjacency List**.

slides.question: Q29: If a research scenario or project requires: **shortest path in unweighted graphs**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of shortest path in unweighted graphs, you should select **Breadth-First Search (BFS)**.

slides.question: Q30: If a research scenario or project requires: **connectivity and topological sorting tool**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of connectivity and topological sorting tool, you should select **Depth-First Search (DFS)**.

slides.question: Q31: If a research scenario or project requires: **atomic column values requirement**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of atomic column values requirement, you should select **First Normal Form (1NF)**.

slides.question: Q32: If a research scenario or project requires: **elimination of partial dependencies**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of elimination of partial dependencies, you should select **Second Normal Form (2NF)**.

slides.question: Q33: If a research scenario or project requires: **elimination of transitive dependencies**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of elimination of transitive dependencies, you should select **Third Normal Form (3NF)**.

slides.question: Q34: If a research scenario or project requires: **eliminating overlapping candidate key anomalies**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of eliminating overlapping candidate key anomalies, you should select **Boyce-Codd Normal Form (BCNF)**.

slides.question: Q35: If a research scenario or project requires: **transaction reliability standards**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of transaction reliability standards, you should select **ACID Properties**.

slides.question: Q36: If a research scenario or project requires: **referential integrity constraint**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of referential integrity constraint, you should select **Foreign Key**.

slides.question: Q37: If a research scenario or project requires: **disk-optimized indexing structure**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of disk-optimized indexing structure, you should select **B+ Tree Index**.

slides.question: Q38: If a research scenario or project requires: **serializability guarantee in transactions**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of serializability guarantee in transactions, you should select **Two-Phase Locking (2PL)**.

slides.question: Q39: If a research scenario or project requires: **multi-table data retrieval operation**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of multi-table data retrieval operation, you should select **SQL JOIN**.

slides.question: Q40: If a research scenario or project requires: **theoretical query language foundation**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of theoretical query language foundation, you should select **Relational Algebra**.

slides.question: Q41: If a research scenario or project requires: **7-layer abstraction framework**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of 7-layer abstraction framework, you should select **OSI Model**.

slides.question: Q42: If a research scenario or project requires: **reliable internet transport framework**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of reliable internet transport framework, you should select **TCP/IP Protocol**.

slides.question: Q43: If a research scenario or project requires: **IP address segmentation tool**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of IP address segmentation tool, you should select **Subnet Mask**.

slides.question: Q44: If a research scenario or project requires: **internet autonomous routing protocol**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of internet autonomous routing protocol, you should select **Border Gateway Protocol (BGP)**.

slides.question: Q45: If a research scenario or project requires: **single shared-key cryptography**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of single shared-key cryptography, you should select **Symmetric Encryption**.

slides.question: Q46: If a research scenario or project requires: **public-private key pair cryptography**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of public-private key pair cryptography, you should select **Asymmetric Encryption**.

slides.question: Q47: If a research scenario or project requires: **internet address resolution directory**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of internet address resolution directory, you should select **DNS (Domain Name System)**.

slides.question: Q48: If a research scenario or project requires: **IP to MAC address resolver**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of IP to MAC address resolver, you should select **ARP (Address Resolution Protocol)**.

slides.question: Q49: If a research scenario or project requires: **traffic filtering barrier**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of traffic filtering barrier, you should select **Firewall**.

slides.question: Q50: If a research scenario or project requires: **128-bit internet address space standard**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of 128-bit internet address space standard, you should select **IPv6 Address Space**.

slides.title: Discussion & Review 💬
slides.content: Review your answers for Mock Exam 6. Pay special attention to topics where you selected incorrect answers!

box.content: You have completed Computer Science Mock Exam 6.
box.note: Solve all 10 mock exams to master the 500 Computer Science questions!
